sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 861301, "Secret Harbor Creek", Stream, Carson City, NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":861301,"gaz_name":"Secret Harbor Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Carson City (city)"} — geometry multipoint; mouth point (Web Mercator -13350877.8059, 4743008.6722) converts to lon -119.932976, lat 39.148802 at the creek's mouth on Lake Tahoe, about 873 m from the file's previously recorded coordinate (-119.92332, 39.14644); corrected to this GNIS-backed mouth coordinate.

parameters.note: Secret Harbor Creek is a west-slope Carson Range stream draining into Secret Harbor on the Nevada east shore of Lake Tahoe (OSM way 543437020; mouth near 39.14904, -119.93236), so it is a Lake Tahoe tributary. NDOW classifies it under Carson City County (Western Region), and it is governed by the Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing in CR 25-16: open year-round, with restricted hours and a 5/10 game-fish limit. This specific sub-listing overrides the Carson City general 'All waters' default (5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish daily; any hour). Carson City has no closed waters in CR 25-16, so no part of this creek is closed — unlike the Washoe County side of Lake Tahoe, where Third, Incline, and Wood creeks are closed. This is the Nevada rule; it differs from the California §7.50(b)(152) seasonal catch-and-release closure that applies to Tahoe tributaries on the California side of the lake.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Carson City — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round as a Lake Tahoe tributary (Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing); Carson City lists no closed waters.
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: The Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing sets 5 game fish daily and 10 in possession, with no species sub-limit. 'Game fish' in Nevada includes trout, mountain whitefish, kokanee salmon, bass, and other listed game species combined.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Carson City — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish daily, 10 in possession (Lake Tahoe and tributaries listing; no species sub-limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 game fish    Possession: 10 game fish

parameters.note: The Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing restricts fishing hours to 1 hour before sunrise through 2 hours after sunset (unlike Carson City's general 'All waters' default, which allows fishing any hour of the day or night).

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Carson City — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

parameters.note: Nevada requires a fishing license for residents and nonresidents 12 years of age or older; anglers under 12 need no license (nonresidents under 12 are limited to 50% of the daily bag). The California/Nevada Lake Tahoe license reciprocity (and the Nevada Interstate Boundary Waters License) applies to fishing IN Lake Tahoe itself, not to its tributaries above the lake — a Nevada fishing license is required to fish Secret Harbor Creek.

regulations.humanSummary: Nevada fishing license required for anglers 12+ (Lake Tahoe license reciprocity does not extend to the tributary).
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
